summary: Excellent soundboard recording with crystal‑clear levels; the show shines thanks to a standout Werewolves of London and an upbeat first set.
review: The tape is a pristine Gans/Betty’s Board capture – levels are perfect, the mix clean and the audience ambience minimal. Musically the concert delivers an energetic first set and a historically interesting second set highlighted by the debut of Warren Zevon’s Werewolves of London, though some listeners note off‑time vocals from Jerry and a weak appearance by Donna Dogchow on The Other One.

ai_rating.rationale: Six detailed reviews give an average of 4.2 stars; the majority praise both performance and recording, with only one negative comment that does not outweigh the overall enthusiasm.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners repeatedly describe the tape as "very clean", "perfect levels" and "crisp"; no significant drop‑outs or muddiness are reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as upbeat and well played; second set noted as interesting with a historic debut of Werewolves of London, though the track list mislabeling (Deal vs Samson) caused minor confusion.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocal timing was off on some verses, but his slide guitar work received praise.
